text.title: django-imports Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-imports as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Importing model classes directly from other apps without aliases: from apps.users.models import User.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Direct unaliased cross-app imports create circular dependency risks and name collision hazards. When two apps both have a User model, the last import wins — silently. Use from apps.users import models as user_models and reference user_models.User everywhere.

failure-mode.description: Using from app.models import * or from app.views import * anywhere in a Django project.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Wildcard imports make it impossible to determine where a name comes from without running the code. They also import private names (_internal), pollute the module namespace, and confuse both human readers and type checkers. Django's autoloading already handles admin.py, apps.py, etc. — wildcards add no value.

failure-mode.description: Adding from __future__ import annotations to DRF serializer or view files that use DRF field introspection or drf-spectacular schema generation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DRF uses get_type_hints() for serializer field resolution. from __future__ import annotations defers all annotations to strings, which breaks DRF's introspection and drf-spectacular's schema generation. The annotations become unparseable strings at the point where DRF expects actual type objects. Verify DRF compatibility before adding __future__ annotations to serializer files.

failure-mode.description: Using lazy from heavy_module import HeavyClass syntax (PEP 810) on Python 3.12 or 3.13 codebases.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: PEP 810 lazy import syntax is not available until Python 3.14+. Using it on 3.12/3.13 causes SyntaxError at import time — not at the usage site. The current alternative (function-scoped import or importlib.import_module()) achieves the same effect without version risk.

failure-mode.description: Using multi-dot relative imports (from ...users.models import User) or other multi-dot relative imports across app boundaries.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multi-dot relative imports break on any directory restructuring and create fragile coupling to the physical directory layout. They also confuse readers about where the module lives. Replace with absolute cross-app imports: from apps.users import models as user_models.
